Each proposal should stem from a simple and elegant question, observation, or hypothesis about the topic. {"idea": {"Name": "geometric_subspace_denoising_icl", "Title": "Geometric Subspace Filtering for Robust In-Context Learning", "Short Hypothesis": "In-context learning relies on a low-rank functional subspace formed by demonstration activations; projecting query activations onto this geometrically isolated subspace at inference time will filter out noise and distributional shifts, significantly improving ICL robustness without any model training. This setting is optimal because simpler interventions (e.g., prompt engineering or 1D contrastive steering) fail to capture the multi-dimensional, entangled nature of ICL representations, while fine-tuning is opaque and computationally prohibitive.
